picks.rationale: The Washington Nationals are facing a significantly depleted Miami Marlins team. The Marlins have multiple key pitchers on the injured list, including Eury Pérez (gracilis strain, expected to miss two months), Andrew Nardi (rib cage stress reaction, out until September), and Janson Junk (shin inflammation). Additionally, outfielder Griffin Conine is on the 60-day IL with a hamstring tear. While the Nationals also have injuries, the Marlins' pitching staff is particularly hit hard, which should give the Nationals an edge at home. The Nationals are 31-29, while the Marlins are 26-34, indicating better overall form for Washington.

The Milwaukee Brewers, with a strong 35-21 record, are hosting the struggling San Francisco Giants (23-36). The Giants are dealing with several key injuries, including starting pitcher Tyler Mahle (hamstring strain) and outfielders Heliot Ramos (quad strain) and Harrison Bader (plantar fasciitis). While the Brewers also have some pitchers on the IL like Brandon Woodruff, their rotation depth has been a strength, with Jacob Misiorowski leading the staff in strikeouts. The Brewers are at home and have a significant advantage in overall team performance and current health of key position players.

The Texas Rangers are severely impacted by injuries to their core offensive players, with Corey Seager (back), Wyatt Langford (forearm), and Josh Smith (glute) all on the 10-day injured list. Evan Carter is also listed as day-to-day with a foot issue, further weakening their lineup. The Cardinals, despite some injuries to Ramón Urías and Lars Nootbaar, have a more stable lineup for this matchup. Given the significant offensive absences for the Rangers, the Cardinals at home present a strong value pick.

The Colorado Rockies are struggling with a 22-38 record and have a lengthy injury list, including key players like Kris Bryant (lumbar degenerative disk disease), Jordan Beck (hamstring), and Mickey Moniak (ankle). Pitchers Jose Quintana (elbow sprain) and Chase Dollander (elbow sprain) are also sidelined. While the Angels also have injuries to Nolan Schanuel (ankle) and Yoán Moncada (knee), their overall roster appears less decimated. The Angels are at home and, despite their own struggles, face a team with even more significant personnel issues, making them a solid favorite.

The Los Angeles Dodgers (38-21) are a dominant team and are facing an Arizona Diamondbacks team with significant injuries. The Diamondbacks are missing key players like Lourdes Gurriel Jr. (hamstring), Carlos Santana (groin), and Corbin Burnes (elbow surgery). While the Dodgers have their own injuries to Teoscar Hernández (hamstring) and Tyler Glasnow (back spasms), their overall depth and recent performance (won Sunday's game 9-1) give them a clear advantage. The Dodgers' strong offense and pitching, even with some absences, should be able to cover the -1.5 spread against a hobbled Diamondbacks squad.
